classObjective: Calculate the slope of a line through two given points and interpret its meaning.
workedExamples: 12-8, Example 1 — Determine Whether Lines Through Given Points Are Parallel, Perpendicular, or Neither
12-8, Example 2 — Determine Whether Graphed Lines Are Parallel, Perpendicular, or Neither
warmUp: Find the slope of the line through (2, 5) and (6, 13)
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: slope formula; rise over run; slope interpretation from graphs; parallel/perpendicular slope relationships
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice calculating slopes and determining parallel/perpendicular relationships
independentPractice: Practice problems calculating slope from points and graphs and interpreting slope meaning
assessment: Exit ticket: calculate the slope of a hill that rises 30 feet over a horizontal distance of 150 feet and explain what it means
capReflection: How does the sign of a slope (positive/negative) describe the direction of a line?
